ACM.core_strength: Computing theory, systems, human-computer interaction, software engineering
best_for_fields: AI/ML with HCI or systems focus
not_ideal_for: Clinical medical studies without CS contribution

IEEE.full_name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
IEEE.core_strength: Engineering, hardware, networks, embedded systems, applied signal processing
best_for_fields: AI in edge/fog computing or medical devices
not_ideal_for: Pure theoretical CS without engineering angle
UX design without technical system integration

Springer.core_strength: Theoretical, mathematical, and foundational computer science; interdisciplinary modeling
not_ideal_for: Empirical UX studies without theoretical framing

Elsevier.core_strength: Applied, interdisciplinary research with real-world impact; strong in domain integration
best_for_fields: AI in Social Sciences (policy, behavior, large-scale data)
not_ideal_for: Low-level protocol specification without application context

Artificial Intelligence (General).note: Depends on focus: theory → Springer; applications → Elsevier; systems/HCI → ACM; edge/embedded → IEEE

Machine Learning.note: Theory → Springer; applied (e.g., healthcare, finance) → Elsevier; systems/ML infrastructure → ACM

Cybersecurity.note: Network/systems security → IEEE; formal methods/crypto → ACM; applied threat analysis in domains → Elsevier

Internet of Things (IoT).note: Hardware, protocols, CPS → IEEE; smart environments with HCI → ACM; societal/health applications → Elsevier

Human-Computer Interaction (HCI).note: Core HCI, UX, accessibility → ACM; behavioral/social impact studies → Elsevier

Software Engineering.note: Testing, debugging, formal methods → ACM; embedded/real-time SE → IEEE; industrial case studies → Elsevier

Computer Networking.note: Protocols, 5G/6G, wireless → IEEE; theoretical algorithms/network theory → ACM

Theoretical Computer Science.note: Algorithms, complexity, logic → Springer (LNCS); applied theory → ACM

Data Science.note: Real-world data applications → Elsevier; scalable systems → ACM; statistical foundations → Springer

Quantum Computing.note: Split by sub-area: ACM for algorithms, IEEE for devices/architectures
